# Coding Agent Project Management MCP Server ★ New (July 14)

Project management built for the AI coding agent era. Manage bugs, features, sprints, and cross-tenant contracts through 71 MCP tools — purpose-built for operators who orchestrate AI-assisted development workflows across multiple agents and projects.

**Source:** mcp.so (exact repo TBD — GitHub search API rate-limited)
**Submitted:** July 14, 2026

## Key Features

- **71 MCP tools:** Comprehensive project management operations accessible from any MCP client
- **Bug tracking:** Create, assign, and track bugs across coding agents
- **Feature management:** Define, prioritize, and track features through development
- **Sprint planning:** Organize work into sprints with agent-aware assignment
- **Cross-tenant contracts:** Manage work across multiple clients or teams
- **Agent-native:** Designed for AI coding agents to interact with, not just humans

## Business Relevance

As AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor) become integral to development workflows, traditional project management tools built for humans don't fit. This MCP server bridges that gap — your AI agents can read tasks, update status, and track progress programmatically while humans maintain visibility through the same interface.

Essential for:
- **Engineering managers** overseeing AI-assisted development teams
- **Agencies** managing multiple client projects with AI coding agents
- **Solo developers** orchestrating multiple AI agents across projects
- **DevOps teams** integrating AI agent workflows into existing pipelines

## Integration with CorpusIQ

Pair with CorpusIQ's project management and time-tracking connectors to connect AI agent activity with business operations. Track agent-assisted development costs against project budgets. Monitor AI coding agent productivity across client engagements.

## Limitations

- Exact GitHub repo TBD (GitHub search API rate-limited as of July 14)
- 71 tools is extensive but may overlap with existing PM tools already in stack
- Adoption requires teams comfortable with AI agents managing project state
- Learning curve for configuring agent-appropriate workflows
